title: Bridge Essay: Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency.
concept: Spontaneous thought — mind-wandering, DMN, creative insight, involuntary cognition.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Non-fabrication** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Non-fabrication — spontaneous thought, mind-wandering, DMN, creative insight.
**Phase context:** Spontaneous thought — mind-wandering, DMN, creative insight, involuntary cognition.

**Personality and complex brain networks: The role of openness to experience in default network efficiency.** — Hum Brain Mapp (2016). DOI: 10.1002/hbm.23065. PMID: 26610181. The brain's default network (DN) has been a topic of considerable empirical interest. In fMRI research, DN activity is associated with spontaneous and self-generated cognition, such as mind-wandering, episodic memory retrieval, future thinking, mental simulation, theory of mind reasoning, and creative cognition. Despite large literatures on developmental and disease-related influences on the DN, surprisingly little is known about the factors that impact normal variation in DN functioning. Using
